title: quercus sessiliflora var. tridactyla
intro: Quercus sessiliflora var. tridactyla, also known as the three-toothed evergreen oak or Tridactyla oak, is a variant of the Quercus sessiliflora tree species, native to Asia and known for its unique three-toothed leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus sessiliflora var. tridactyla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support cardiovascular health, among others. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, or extracts, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
